goal: v2.0 substrate cutover: retire src/cli/slop, decompose under src/slop/{tree,language,structure,lexicon,linter,cli,config,schemas,app}; deletion sweep retires SLOP_ENGINE toggle, engine.py, models.py, Violation; per-package rule registries aggregate at slop.linter

summary.title: v2.0 substrate cutover — decompose monolithic slop package
summary.what: Replaced the monolithic src/cli/slop package with a substrate-aligned decomposition under src/slop: tree (parse foundation, Codebase → Tree), language (ABC + paradigm subclasses + 11 grammar configs), structure (Structure view + 20 structural rules including information.*), lexicon (Lexicon view + 9 lexical rules), linter (Linter + Result + Slop + 2 cross-cutting rules + dispatch + format), cli (per-command split with cmd.py convention + install subgroup), config (loader + models), schemas (IO generation), app.py composition root. Deletion sweep retired the SLOP_ENGINE=v2 environment toggle, slop.engine, slop.models, slop.output, slop.rules central registry, the Violation type (renamed Slop), and the RuleDefinition.run_v2 slot. Per-package rule registries (STRUCTURAL_RULES, LEXICAL_RULES, CROSS_CUTTING_RULES) now aggregate at slop.linter.RULE_REGISTRY. New substrate tests under src/tests/test_v2 (~77 tests across 17 files) exercise the v2 stack independently of legacy paths. All 473 tests pass; CLI entry moves from slop.cli:main to slop.app:main.
summary.why: The v1.x monolith mixed parse substrate, metric kernels, rule wrappers, and CLI surface inside one src/cli/slop package, with discovery primitives split by tool (_fs/_text/_ast). The v2.0 decomposition reorganises by responsibility instead of by substrate-tool, isolating the Tree/Language/Structure/Lexicon view hierarchy so future rule ports can extend the views directly. The deletion sweep retires the parity-gate plumbing (SLOP_ENGINE toggle, run_v2 dual-slot) that was only needed during the rule-port window.
summary.user_goal: v2.0 substrate cutover: retire src/cli/slop, decompose under src/slop/{tree,language,structure,lexicon,linter,cli,config,schemas,app}; deletion sweep retires SLOP_ENGINE toggle, engine.py, models.py, Violation; per-package rule registries aggregate at slop.linter

decisions.chose: Bundle the cutover into one commit (9538481) rather than incrementalising it.
decisions.rationale: Intermediate states would have had failing tests (package path change is atomic — slop.app:main vs slop.cli:main can't coexist coherently). Commit 1 (9917743) covers supporting infra; commit 2 covers the cutover itself.

decisions.chose: Retain legacy kernels under src/slop/{_structural,_lexical,_compose,_text,_fs,_ast,_util}; 29 of 31 rules still call them via legacy_v2_shim.
decisions.rationale: Kernel inlining + view-method growth is ~29 rule rewrites with per-rule parity verification — multi-week scope. Cutover scope is the substrate decomposition, not the kernel retirement.

decisions.chose: Per-substrate rule registries (STRUCTURAL_RULES / LEXICAL_RULES / CROSS_CUTTING_RULES) aggregated at slop.linter.RULE_REGISTRY, not a central slop.rules module.
decisions.rationale: A central registry duplicates the substrate boundary and re-introduces the monolith shape the v2.0 decomposition eliminates. Co-locating rules with their view is the cleaner ISP-aligned layout.

decisions.chose: cmd.py convention recursively for every CLI sub-package's root command (slop/cli/cmd.py, slop/cli/install/cmd.py).
decisions.rationale: Matches the user's standing convention across projects; makes the dispatch graph predictable for any future per-subcommand grouping (install, etc.).

decisions.chose: Result.json() returns dict; Result.pretty() returns str; both delegate to slop.linter.format.
decisions.rationale: Separates data shape from serialisation. CLI does json.dumps at the output boundary. Format module owns all rendering choices in one place.

decisions.chose: Rename Result.violation_count → slop_count, Result.result → verdict; no backwards-compat aliases.
decisions.rationale: Clean v2.0 break: solo project, no external Python API consumers, no PR review surface. Aliases would just be dead weight to maintain.

review_notes: 473 tests pass; CLI tested manually via 'slop rules' (31 entries) and 'slop check structural.complexity --root slop'.
Validation against parity gate: prior to deletion sweep, all 31 rules had run_v2 set and produced equivalent JSON output to legacy via scripts/diff_results.py.
PyPI entry point changed from slop.cli:main to slop.app:main; 'slop --help' confirms argparse dispatch resolves correctly.

Stale mainline proposals (int_367818ce, int_0eda73c7, int_95544d3c) from prior feature branches (feature/lexicon-port, lexicon-affix-noise, lexicon-hygiene) still show 'proposed' status. Their corresponding commits already landed on dev (1c50216, 318f1bf, and predecessors). Recommend 'mainline doctor --proposals' to reconcile.

architectural_claims: Single-responsibility decomposition: slop/{tree,language,structure,lexicon,linter,cli,config,schemas,app} with each package owning one concern and one public surface.
View pattern: Structure and Lexicon are read-only views over a parsed Tree. Rules consume views; rules do not parse code themselves.
Per-substrate rule registries aggregated at the linter, not a central rules module. Each substrate's rules live under <substrate>/rules/.
Composition root at slop.app:main; slop.cli only owns argparse + dispatch.
cmd.py convention: every CLI sub-package's root command lives in cmd.py.
Slop is the canonical finding type (renamed from Violation); Result is the canonical run output (replaces LintResult); both live in slop.linter.
behavioral_changes: SLOP_ENGINE=v2 environment toggle removed; v2 is the only execution path.
Result.json() now returns dict (was string); CLI does json.dumps at boundary.
Result.violation_count → Result.slop_count; Result.result → Result.verdict. No alias compatibility retained.
migration_notes: Downstream consumers: update 'from slop.models import X' → split between slop.config.models (config dataclasses) and slop.linter.types (rule types).
Downstream consumers: 'from slop.rules import RULE_REGISTRY' → 'from slop.linter import RULE_REGISTRY'.
Downstream consumers: 'from slop.engine import run_lint' is gone; use 'Linter(config).run()' from slop.linter.linter.
Violation → Slop rename: 'from slop.models import Violation' → 'from slop.linter import Slop'.
SLOP_ENGINE=v2 environment variable no longer has any effect; remove from CI.
